channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Pennsylvania father, sons accused of poaching nearly 50 deer
item.description: An investigation that lasted almost two years found that the Nelsons had been illegally taking whitetail bucks for several years, according to state game wardens.
item.dc:creator: Rian Bossler, NewsNation

item.title: DMV seeing fewer fake IDs
item.description: “We are happy to say that these numbers have been declining in recent years,” said DMV Commissioner Mark J.F. Schroeder.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Republican NY-18 candidate Alison Esposito says America doesn’t have a gun problem
item.description: “We have a heart problem in this country and a mental health problem in this country. We don’t have a gun problem in this country,” Esposito said.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, NewsNation

item.title: Disney World and other Orlando parks to reopen Friday after Hurricane Milton shutdown
item.description: Florida theme parks including Disney World, Universal Orlando and SeaWorld say they’ll open their gates on Friday after shutting down for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Milton by the numbers: At least 5 dead, at least 12 tornadoes, 3.4M without power
item.description: Hurricane Milton's explosive formation stirred high anxiety for Florida less than two weeks after Helene raked the state and much of the Southeast. On Thursday, authorities were still assessing the damage, even as Gov. Ron DeSantis said it fell short of “the worst-case scenario.
item.dc:creator: MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Milton by the numbers: At least 5 dead, at least 12 tornadoes, 3.4M without power
item.description: Hurricane Milton’s explosive formation stirred high anxiety for Florida less than two weeks after Helene raked the state and much of the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: Mary Katherine Wildeman And Christopher L. Keller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris faces new urgency to explain how her potential presidency would be different from Biden's
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With less than four weeks until Election Day, Vice President Kamala Harris is facing new urgency to define how her potential presidency would be different from that of President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's small-dollar donor fundraising is beset by confusion and fatigue
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump’s contributions from small-dollar donors have plummeted since his last bid for the White House, presenting the former president with a financial challenge as he attempts to keep pace with Democrats' fundraising machine.
item.dc:creator: DAN MERICA, AARON KESSLER and RICHARD LARDNER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: TD Bank to pay $3B, face growth cap over money laundering violations
item.description: Toronto-Dominion Bank, commonly referred to as TD Bank, pleaded guilty Thursday to violating federal anti-money laundering and bank transparency laws, agreeing to pay more than $3 billion and limit its future growth.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Milton and Helene leave students, schools reeling
item.description: As Hurricane Milton slams into Florida — and amid Helene's lingering destruction — students across the southeast are facing severe disruptions to their academic year, posing challenges for both educators and children after they have already witnessed devastation in their communities.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Maryland candidates debate abortion rights in widely watched US Senate race
item.description: Democrat Angela Alsobrooks is highlighting former Gov. Larry Hogan’s actions in office as evidence he isn’t as supportive of abortion rights as he now claims to be.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hurricane Milton disrupts Yom Kippur plans for Jews in Florida
item.description: WINTER PARK, Florida (AP) — Many Jews worldwide will mark Yom Kippur in fasting and prayer at their synagogues this weekend.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO and HOLLY MEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican and Democratic House Latinos face off in race to increase their numbers
item.description: Latino House Republicans are building out campaign infrastructure to protect their members and grow their numbers, at times directly competing with their Democratic counterparts, who have a huge head start. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Police rescue 15 people from Tampa home after Milton knocks tree on house
item.description: Officers with the Tampa Police Department were sheltering a few miles when they were alerted to the incident via a 911 call.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, KRON4

item.title: Road workers arrested after claiming cop attacked them
item.description: Contracted road workers say they are facing felony assault and kidnapping charges after claiming they were the ones assaulted by a mystery driver.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Milton knocks out power to millions and spawns slew of tornadoes. At least 5 dead
item.description: Hurricane Milton barreled into the Atlantic Ocean on Thursday after plowing across Florida, where it knocked out power to more than 3 million customers and whipped up a barrage of tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rafael Nadal, 22-time Grand Slam tennis champ, announces retirement
item.description: Rafael Nadal, professional tennis legend and 22-time Grand Slam champion announced his retirement in a video message early Thursday morning.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Milton knocks out power to millions and spawns slew of tornadoes. At least 5 dead
item.description: Hurricane Milton barreled into the Atlantic Ocean on Thursday after plowing across Florida, where it knocked out power to more than 3 million customers and whipped up a barrage of tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Arizona Senate debate
item.description: Rep. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) and Republican candidate Kari Lake faced off in their only debate in the Arizona Senate race Wednesday, and the two contenders frequently traded barbs. Gallego and Lake, who are vying for retiring Sen.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Jenna Fischer of 'The Office' reveals breast cancer diagnosis, recovery
item.description: Jenna Fischer, the actress well-known for her role as Pam on "The Office," revealed her diagnosis and recovery in a post to social media.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: State police recruit's death in Massachusetts overshadows graduation ceremony
item.description: WORCESTER, Mass. (AP) — Holding signs demanding truth and chanting for justice, several dozen people gathered Wednesday outside a graduation ceremony for Massachusetts State Police cadets demanding an explanation of how one of the recruits died during a training exercise.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Landfall timeline: When will Hurricane Milton hit Florida?
item.description: Hurricane Milton continues to push closer to the Florida coastline, and the impacts begin well before landfall.
item.dc:creator: Leigh Spann, KRON4

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, ABC 4

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, widow of Robert F. Kennedy, suffers stroke
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The family of Ethel Kennedy, the widow of Robert F. Kennedy, says she's been hospitalized after suffering a stroke.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Whistleblower claims Secret Service 'painting a false picture' of protection for Trump: Hawley
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley, R-Mo., is detailing new whistleblower allegations that Secret Service leadership is trying to hide the level of protection given to former President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plane makes emergency landing in New York City after pilot dies mid-flight
item.description: Crew members on the Turkish Airlines flight worked to revive the pilot, but he died before the plane landed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Over 9 million pounds of ready-to-eat meat, poultry recalled: USDA
item.description: The products may be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes, according to the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, ABC 4

item.title: Missouri Ozarks pastor charged with murder shot wife's lover at point-blank range: police
item.description: Missouri pastor Matthew Dedmon allegedly shot Joe Newburn in the chest in 2022 after seeing him in a car with his wife. Now, he has been granted bond while he awaits trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, widow of Robert F. Kennedy, hospitalized after suffering stroke, family says
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, is former President John F. Kennedy's sister-in-law and mother to 11 children, including former independent presidential candidate Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Beloved father fighting for life in ICU after house explosion in Long Beach
item.description: The man injured in a violent house explosion on Monday is fighting for his life in the intensive care unit, his family tells KTLA.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5

item.title: Harris reveals owning a Glock: 'Of course' I've fired it
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris said in a "60 Minutes" interview that aired Monday that she owns a Glock and has fired it at a shooting range in another example of how she and her campaign have made a point to highlight her gun ownership.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Sally Field shares 'horrific' teenage abortion experience: 'These are the things that women are going through now'
item.description: Sally Field is opening up about her "horrific" experience of being molested while undergoing an abortion in Mexico as a teenager, saying the traumatic event is one of the reasons she's supporting Vice President Harris's White House bid.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: When will we 'fall back?' What Utahns have tried to get daylight saving time permanent
item.description: It is almost time to fall back, the first weekend of November marks the end of daylight saving across much of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4

item.title: FEMA administrator continues pushback against false claims as Helene death toll hits 230
item.description: LAKE LURE, N.C. (AP) — The head of the U.S. disaster response agency continued to forcefully push back Monday against false claims and conspiracy theories about her agency’s response to Hurricane Helene as the death toll from the storm continued to climb.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY and GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Are hurricanes intensifying more rapidly than ever before?
item.description: Rapid intensification is defined by the National Hurricane Center as an increase of at least 30 knots (about 35 mph) in maximum sustained winds within a 24-hour period, which can mean a jump of two categories on the Saffir-Simpson scale.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Hurricane Helene aftermath poses election hurdles in critical battlegrounds
item.description: Hurricane Helene has thrown up new hurdles for voters and election officials alike in Georgia and North Carolina, threatening disruptions to the voting process in two of the most critical battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: 3 Florida cities home to the worst drivers in the U.S., study says
item.description: A Tennessee city has once again been given the dubious title of home to the worst drivers in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC
